title: Early Intervention
subtitle: (1–3 Years)
shortDescription: Targeted support during the most critical window of brain development for children aged 1–3 years.
description: The first three years of life are the most important for brain development. Our Early Intervention program identifies developmental delays early and provides structured, play-based therapy to help young children reach their milestones. We work closely with families to ensure progress continues at home.
benefits: Parent coaching and home programme guidance
Regular progress monitoring and IEP updates

title: Occupational Therapy
shortDescription: Helping children develop the essential skills needed for daily living, learning, and independence.
description: Occupational Therapy at Creating Links focuses on building functional independence in children. Our certified occupational therapists assess each child's unique needs and design personalised programmes targeting fine motor skills, self-care, handwriting, play skills, and daily routines.
benefits: Fine motor and hand strength development
Play skill development and social participation

title: Sensory Integration
shortDescription: Helping children process and respond to sensory information from their environment more effectively.
description: Many children struggle to process sensory information — sounds, touch, movement, or textures — in a typical way. Our Sensory Integration therapy uses structured, play-based activities to help the brain organise sensory input, reducing overreactions or under-responses that affect behaviour and learning.
benefits: Better tolerance to touch, sound, and movement
Calmer, more adaptive behaviour in daily life

title: Beyond Behavioral Therapy: Complete Psychological Assessment & Care
shortDescription: Comprehensive psychological assessment and evidence-based strategies to understand, support, and positively shape children's behaviour and emotional wellbeing.
description: Our Behavioral Therapy programme uses evidence-based approaches to help children understand their emotions, manage challenging behaviours, and develop positive social interactions. We work with families to implement consistent strategies at home, school, and in the community.
benefits: Social skills and peer interaction training

title: Speech & Language Therapy
shortDescription: Building communication skills through targeted speech, language, and AAC interventions.
description: We provide individualised, child-centred therapy with tailored intervention plans designed to meet each child's unique needs. Our approach integrates evidence-based practices, incorporating tools such as the Oral Placement Therapy (OPT) approach and Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) systems, including apps like Avaz and low-tech communication boards, to support functional and meaningful communication development.
benefits: AAC support: Avaz app and low-tech communication boards

title: Group Therapy
shortDescription: Structured group sessions that build social skills, peer interaction, and teamwork in a safe setting.
description: Group Therapy at Creating Links brings children together in small, structured groups to practise real-world social skills. Sessions are facilitated by our therapists and include cooperative activities, educational outings, and peer learning opportunities that prepare children for inclusive environments.
benefits: Generalisation of skills to real-world settings
Small group sizes for focused support

title: School Readiness Program
shortDescription: A structured programme preparing children for mainstream or NIOS school environments at their own pace.
description: Our School Readiness Program bridges the gap between home and school using a three-level framework — Wings to Fly, Ready to Soar, and Sky's the Limit. We address imitation, communication, cognitive skills, social-emotional regulation, and daily living skills using a data-driven, IEP-based approach.
benefits: Three-level programme: Wings to Fly → Ready to Soar → Sky's the Limit
Individualised Education Plans (IEP) for every child
Yoga, educational trips, and group learning included
Pathway to mainstream or NIOS school integration

title: Special Education
shortDescription: Personalised academic and life skills instruction adapted to each child's unique learning profile.
description: Our Special Education programme provides individualised academic instruction for children with learning disabilities, intellectual disabilities, autism, and other developmental conditions. Our special educators use multi-sensory teaching methods, assistive tools, and differentiated curriculum to help each child succeed.
benefits: Curriculum adapted to individual learning level
Reading, writing, and numeracy skill building

title: Physiotherapy
shortDescription: Improving movement, strength, and physical function to help children participate fully in everyday life.
description: Our paediatric physiotherapy programme addresses gross motor delays, muscle tone issues, coordination difficulties, and postural concerns. Therapists design movement-based programmes that improve physical strength, balance, and mobility — enabling children to play, explore, and participate in school activities.

title: Vocational Training
shortDescription: Life skills and pre-vocational programmes that build independence and confidence in older children and young adults.
description: Our Vocational Training programme prepares older children and young adults with special needs for greater independence. Through practical life-skills activities — including our signature Fearless Cooking sessions, hygiene routines, money management, and community participation — we build the functional skills needed for adult life.
benefits: Practical cooking and kitchen safety skills
Money handling and basic financial literacy
